description: An extension of the Flexible Collision Library.
maintainers: Joseph Mirabel, Justin Carpentier, Louis Montaut, Wolfgang Merkt, Guilhem Saurel
readme: Coal extension Flexible Collision Library FCL forked creating project called HPP-FCL. large part code rewritten removed (unused untested code), features introduced Due major decided rename HPP-FCL project Coal. Coal projects research papers, cite features Compared original FCL library, main features dedicated efficient implementations GJK EPA algorithms rely libccd) support safety margins collision detection accelerated version collision detection Nesterov, leads increased performance factor details paper computation bound distance objects collision checking performed, collision implementation Python bindings easy code prototyping support geometries height fields, capsules, ellipsoids, enhance reliability fix myriad bugs efficient computation contact points contact patches objects full support object serialization Boost.Serialization Note: broad phase reintroduced Justin Carpentier based FCL version project robotics frameworks Pinocchio, open-source library implements efficient versatile rigid-body dynamics algorithms, Humanoid Path Planner, open-source library Motion Manipulation Planning. Coal develop Simple, (differentiable) efficient simulator robotics high-performance library original FCL library, Coal implements well-established GJK algorithm variants collision detection distance computation. implementations lead state-of-the-art performance, shown figures hand, benchmarked Coal major state-of-the-art software alternatives: Bullet simulator, original FCL library Drake framework), libccd library MuJoCo). results depicted figure, notably shows accelerated variants GJK largely outperform large margin times faster). notice y-axis log scale. hand, care dedicated collision detection solvers GJK narrow phase? simply formulate collision detection problem quadratic problem call off-the-shelf optimization solver ProxQP)? observe GJK-based approaches largely outperform solutions based classic optimization solvers solver ProxQP), notably large geometries composed tens hundreds vertices. Open-source projects relying Coal Pinocchio fast flexible implementation Rigid Body Dynamics algorithms analytical derivatives. IfcOpenShell Open source IFC library geometry engine. Crocoddyl software realize model predictive control complex robotics platforms. TSID software implements Task Space Inverse Dynamics HPP SDK implements motion planners humanoids robots. Jiminy simulator based Pinocchio. ocs2 toolbox Optimal Control Switched Systems (OCS2) Installation Conda Coal installed conda-forge channel: conda install coal conda-forge Build find build instruction library python bindings installed simply conda conda-forge install coal. library, include files python bindings installed $CONDA_PREFIX/lib, $CONDA_PREFIX/include $CONDA_PREFIX/lib/python3.XX/site-packages. Coal #include "coal/math/transform.h" #include "coal/mesh_loader/loader.h" #include "coal/BVH/BVH_model.h" #include "coal/collision.h" #include "coal/collision_data.h" #include <iostream> #include <memory> Function load convex mesh `.obj`, `.stl` `.dae` file. function imports object file BVHModel, point cloud hierarchically transformed tree bounding volumes. leaves tree individual points point cloud stored `.obj` file. BVH collision detection. computational efficiency, prefer work convex hull point cloud. insures underlying object convex fast collision detection algorithms GJK EPA called object. creating BVH structure point cloud, function computes convex hull. std::shared_ptr<coal::ConvexBase> loadConvexMesh(const std::string& file_name) coal::NODE_TYPE bv_type coal::BV_AABB; coal::MeshLoader loader(bv_type); coal::BVHModelPtr_t bvh loader.load(file_name); bvh->buildConvexHull(true, return bvh->convex; int main() Create coal shapes. Coal supports primitive shapes: boxes, spheres, capsules, cylinders, ellipsoids, cones, planes, halfspace convex meshes convex hulls clouds points). supports BVHs (bounding volumes hierarchies), height-fields octrees. std::shared_ptr<coal::Ellipsoid> shape1 std::make_shared<coal::Ellipsoid>(0.7, std::shared_ptr<coal::ConvexBase> shape2 loadConvexMesh("../path/to/mesh/file.obj"); Define shapes' placement space coal::Transform3s T1.setQuatRotation(coal::Quaternion3f::UnitRandom()); T1.setTranslation(coal::Vec3s::Random()); coal::Transform3s coal::Transform3s::Identity(); T2.setQuatRotation(coal::Quaternion3f::UnitRandom()); T2.setTranslation(coal::Vec3s::Random()); Define collision requests results. collision request set parameters collision pair. set positive negative security margin. distance shapes security margin, shapes considered collision. Setting positive security margin usefull motion planning, prevent shapes close physics simulation, allowing negative security margin usefull stabilize simulation. coal::CollisionRequest col_req; col_req.security_margin collision result stores result collision test (signed distance shapes, witness points location, normal coal::CollisionResult col_res; Collision call coal::collide(shape1.get(), shape2.get(), col_req, col_res); access collision result populated std::cout "Collision? col_res.isCollision() (col_res.isCollision()) coal::Contact contact col_res.getContact(0); penetration depth account security margin. penetration depth true signed distance separates shapes. distance takes account security margin, simply add std::cout "Penetration depth: contact.penetration_depth std::cout "Distance shapes including security margin: contact.penetration_depth col_req.security_margin std::cout "Witness point shape1: contact.nearest_points[0].transpose() std::cout "Witness point shape2: contact.nearest_points[1].transpose() std::cout "Normal: contact.normal.transpose() calling collision test, important clear previous results stored collision result. col_res.clear(); return Python translated python python bindings Coal: import numpy import coal Optional: Pinocchio library rigid body algorithms library handy module. installed simply `conda conda-forge install pinocchio`. Installing pinocchio installs coal. import pinocchio pin def loadConvexMesh(file_name: str): loader coal.MeshLoader() bvh: coal.BVHModelBase loader.load(file_name) bvh.buildConvexHull(True, return bvh.convex "__main__": Create coal shapes shape1 coal.Ellipsoid(0.7, shape2 loadConvexMesh("../path/to/mesh/file.obj") Define shapes' placement space coal.Transform3s() T1.setTranslation(pin.SE3.Random().translation) T1.setRotation(pin.SE3.Random().rotation) coal.Transform3s(); arrays works T1.setTranslation(np.random.rand(3)) T2.setRotation(pin.SE3.Random().rotation) Define collision requests results col_req coal.CollisionRequest() col_res coal.CollisionResult() Collision call coal.collide(shape1, shape2, col_req, col_res) Accessing collision result populated print("Is collision? {col_res.isCollision()}) col_res.isCollision(): contact: coal.Contact col_res.getContact(0) print("Penetration depth: contact.penetration_depth) print("Distance shapes including security margin: contact.penetration_depth col_req.security_margin) print("Witness point shape1: contact.getNearestPoint1()) print("Witness point shape2: contact.getNearestPoint2()) print("Normal: contact.normal) running collision call, important clear col_res.clear() Acknowledgments development Coal actively supported Gepetto team @LAAS-CNRS, Willow team @INRIA extent, Eureka Robotics.

description: ROS 2 package providing a hardware interface for controlling Dynamixel motors via the ROS 2 control framework.
authors: Hye-Jong KIM, Sungho Woo, Woojin Wie, Wonho Yun
readme: Dynamixel Hardware Interface User Guide Introduction ROS package providing hardware interface controlling Dynamixel motors ros2_control framework. repository includes dynamixel_hardware_interface plugin seamless integration ROS control, dynamixel_interfaces package custom message definitions interface Prerequisites package supports ROS Humble Ensure ROS Humble properly installed (ROS Humble installation guide). Hardware Requirements: Dynamixel servos USB2 Dynamixel adapter Proper power supply Dynamixel motors Installation Clone repository ROS workspace: ~/${WORKSPACE}/src git clone humble https://github.com/ROBOTIS-GIT/DynamixelSDK.git git clone humble https://github.com/ROBOTIS-GIT/dynamixel_hardware_interface.git git clone humble https://github.com/ROBOTIS-GIT/dynamixel_interfaces.git Build package: ~/${WORKSPACE} colcon build Source workspace: source ~/${WORKSPACE}/install/setup.bash Packages project integrates ROS packages provide extended functionality: open_manipulator_x ROS-based open-source software package designed Open Manipulator-X, 4-DOF robotic arm. essential features motion planning, kinematics, control utilities seamless integration ROS environments. open_manipulator_y ROS-based package tailored Open Manipulator-Y, 6-DOF robotic arm. package offers enhanced compatibility extended functionalities advanced manipulator control operations. Configuration effectively Dynamixel Hardware Interface ROS control system, configure specific parameters ros2_control hardware description file. concise explanation key parameters, illustrated examples OpenManipulator-X ROS control.xacro file. Port Settings: Define serial port baud rate communication. Hardware Setup: Configure joints transmissions. Joints: Control monitor robot joints. GPIO: Define control Dynamixel motors. Port Communication Settings parameters define interface communicates Dynamixel motors: port_name: Serial port communication. baud_rate: Communication baud rate. error_timeout_ms: Timeout communication errors milliseconds). Hardware Configuration parameters define hardware setup: number_of_joints: Total number joints. number_of_transmissions: Number transmissions. Transmission Matrices: Define joint-to-transmission mappings. Joint Configuration Joints define control state interfaces robot movement: Key Attributes Unique joint ${prefix}joint1 Sub-Elements <command_interface>: Sends commands joints. <command_interface name="position"> <state_interface>: Monitors joint state data. <state_interface name="position"/> <state_interface name="velocity"/> <state_interface name="effort"/> GPIO Configuration GPIO tag define configuration Dynamixel motors robotics system. serves declarative structure set motor-specific parameters, command interfaces, state monitoring capabilities. seamless integration Dynamixel hardware software frameworks. Key Attributes unique identifier motor configuration dxl1). unique assigned motor Dynamixel network Sub-Elements <param>: Specifies motor-specific settings. parameters correspond properties Dynamixel motor, type, control mode, PID gain values. <param name="type">dxl</param> <command_interface>: Defines control commands motor. setting desired goal position. <command_interface name="Goal Position"/> <state_interface>: Specifies state feedback interfaces monitor real-time motor data, position, velocity, current. <state_interface name="Present Position"/> <state_interface name="Present Velocity"/> <state_interface name="Present Current"/> GPIO Configuration fully defined GPIO configuration Dynamixel motor. demonstrates configure motor define command interfaces, monitor state data, set additional parameters PID gains drive mode. <gpio name="dxl1"> <param name="type">dxl</param> <param name="ID">11</param> <command_interface name="Goal Position"/> <state_interface name="Present Position"/> <state_interface name="Present Velocity"/> <state_interface name="Present Current"/> <param name="Position Gain">800</param> Proportional gain position control <param name="Position Gain">100</param> Integral gain position control <param name="Position Gain">100</param> Derivative gain position control <param name="Drive Mode">0</param> Clockwise, Counterclockwise </gpio> Dynamixel Control Table Reference Dynamixel hardware interface control tables, defined model-specific files xm430_w350.model, configure interact motor’s internal settings. control tables map hardware parameters specific memory addresses data types, enabling fine-grained control monitoring. xm430_w350.model: [Control Table] Address Size Data Model Number Model Information Firmware Version Usage control table specifies internal memory layout Dynamixel motor. instance, set motor address configure firmware-specific options address settings defined GPIO configuration dynamically updated commands based control table schema. professional explanation highlights flexibility precision Dynamixel hardware interface, empowering developers fully utilize motor’s capabilities structured framework. details, refer official Dynamixel e-Manual. Usage Ensure parameters configured correctly ros2_control YAML file XML launch file. Parameter Configuration <ros2_control> <param name="dynamixel_state_pub_msg_name">dynamixel_hardware_interface/dxl_state</param> <param name="get_dynamixel_data_srv_name">dynamixel_hardware_interface/get_dxl_data</param> <param name="set_dynamixel_data_srv_name">dynamixel_hardware_interface/set_dxl_data</param> <param name="reboot_dxl_srv_name">dynamixel_hardware_interface/reboot_dxl</param> <param name="set_dxl_torque_srv_name">dynamixel_hardware_interface/set_dxl_torque</param> </ros2_control> Topic Service Descriptions dynamixel_state_pub_msg_name Description: Defines topic publishing Dynamixel state. Default dynamixel_hardware_interface/dxl_state get_dynamixel_data_srv_name Description: Specifies service retrieving Dynamixel data. Default dynamixel_hardware_interface/get_dxl_data set_dynamixel_data_srv_name Description: Specifies service setting Dynamixel data. Default dynamixel_hardware_interface/set_dxl_data reboot_dxl_srv_name Description: Specifies service rebooting Dynamixel motors. Default dynamixel_hardware_interface/reboot_dxl set_dxl_torque_srv_name Description: Specifies service enabling disabling torque Dynamixel motors. Default dynamixel_hardware_interface/set_dxl_torque Contributing contributions! follow guidelines CONTRIBUTING.md submit issues pull requests. License project licensed MIT License. readme: laser_segmentation Overview Implementation algorithms segmentation laserscans, splitting subsets beams, ROS2 interface. implemented algorithm Jump distance clustering: widely method laser range data mobile robotics. simple fast method segment scans: Euclidean distance adjacent beams exceeds threshold distance, segment generated. Jump distance clustering merge: Similar algorithm checks pre-predecessor segments close deals over-segmented data small cluster outdoor environment. threshold condition implementation mentioned jump distance threshold fixed. dynamic jump distance threshold calculated methods Lee (Lee, Dietmayer (Dietmayer, Santos (Santos, parameters list change Includes dynamic reconfigure server parameter change online configuration algorithms filtering. Laser scan Segments Keywords: ROS2, laser, segmentation, clustering Author: Alberto Tudela laser_segmentation package tested ROS2 Jazzy Ubuntu research code, expect fitness purpose disclaimed. Installation Binaries Ubuntu install latest version package command sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install ros-jazzy-laser-segmentation Building Source Dependencies Robot Operating System (ROS) (middleware robotics), slg_msgs (Library messages interact laser related geometry Jazzy branch), Building build source, clone latest version main repository colcon workspace compile package colcon_workspace/src git clone https://github.com/ajtudela/laser_segmentation.git jazzy rosdep install --from-path src --rosdistro jazzy colcon build --symlink-install Usage scan source running, run laser_segmentation node ros2 launch laser_segmentation segmentation.launch.py Nodes laser_segmentation Segmentation laserscans. Subscribed Topics scan (sensor_msgs/LaserScan) Laser scan topic segmentation performed. Published Topics segments (slg_msgs/SegmentArray) Splitted segments resulting segmentation. segments/visualization (visualization_msgs/MarkerArray) comprises namespaces: “segments”: markers segments showing segments Rviz2. “segments_names”: markers segments. “centroids”: markers segment centroids Rviz2. Parameters scan_topic (string, default: “scan”) Topic laserscan. segment_topic (string, default: “segments”) Topic segmented laser scan. segmentation_type (string, default: “jump_distance”) Choose segmentation algorithms. Jump distance clustering (jump_distance) jump distance merge (jump_distance_merge). Parameters filtering min_points_segment (int, default: Minimium number points segment. max_points_segment (int, default: Maximum number points segment. min_avg_distance_from_sensor (double, default: Minimium average distance sensor. max_avg_distance_from_sensor (double, default: Maximum average distance sensor. min_segment_width (double, default: Minimium width segment. max_segment_width (double, default: Maximum width segment. Parameters segmentation algorithms method_threshold (string, default: Method calculate dynamic jump distance threshold jump_distance jump_distance_merge algorithms. methods based lee (Lee, diet (Dietmayer, santos (Santos, distance_threshold (double, default: algorithms mentioned selected, parameters jump distance segment created. noise_reduction (double, default: Parameter noise reduction “Santos” “Dietmayer” algorithms.

description: Tutorial for point_cloud_transport.
readme: <POINT CLOUD TRANSPORT TUTORIAL> ROS2 Contents Writing Simple Publisher Code Publisher Code Explained Running Publisher Writing Simple Subscriber Code Subscriber Code Explained Running Subscriber Publishers Subscribers Plugins Running Publisher Subsriber Changing Transport Changing Transport Behavior Implementing Custom Plugins Writing Simple Publisher section, create publisher node, opens ROS bag publishes PointCloud2 messages tutorial assumes created workspace [](https://github.com/ros-perception/point_cloud_transport) [](https://github.com/ros-perception/point_cloud_transport_plugins) start, change directory, clone repository, unzip rosbag resources folder: ~/<point_cloud_transport_ws>/src git clone https://github.com/ros-perception/point_cloud_transport_tutorial point_cloud_transport_tutorial tar resources/ -xvf resources/rosbag2_2023_08_05-16_08_51.tar.xz ~/<point_cloud_transport_ws> colcon build --merge-install --event-handlers console_direct+ Code Publisher my_publisher.cpp #include <point_cloud_transport/point_cloud_transport.hpp> reading rosbag #include <ament_index_cpp/get_package_share_directory.hpp> #include <rclcpp/serialization.hpp> #include <rclcpp/rclcpp.hpp> #include <rosbag2_cpp/reader.hpp> #include <rosbag2_cpp/storage_options.hpp> #include <rosbag2_cpp/converter_interfaces/serialization_format_converter.hpp> #include <sensor_msgs/msg/point_cloud2.hpp> int main(int argc, char argv) rclcpp::init(argc, argv); auto node std::make_shared<rclcpp::Node>("point_cloud_publisher"); point_cloud_transport::PointCloudTransport pct(node); point_cloud_transport::Publisher pub pct.advertise("pct/point_cloud", const std::string bagged_cloud_topic "/point_cloud"; const std::string shared_directory ament_index_cpp::get_package_share_directory( "point_cloud_transport_tutorial"); const std::string bag_file shared_directory "/resources/rosbag2_2023_08_05-16_08_51"; boiler-plate rosbag2 read bag rosbag2_storage::StorageOptions storage_options; storage_options.uri bag_file; storage_options.storage_id "mcap"; rosbag2_cpp::ConverterOptions converter_options; converter_options.input_serialization_format "cdr"; converter_options.output_serialization_format "cdr"; open rosbag rosbag2_cpp::readers::SequentialReader reader; reader.open(storage_options, converter_options); sensor_msgs::msg::PointCloud2 cloud_msg; rclcpp::Serialization<sensor_msgs::msg::PointCloud2> cloud_serialization; (reader.has_next() rclcpp::ok()) serialized data auto serialized_message reader.read_next(); rclcpp::SerializedMessage extracted_serialized_msg(*serialized_message->serialized_data); (serialized_message->topic_name bagged_cloud_topic) deserialize convert message cloud_serialization.deserialize_message(&extracted_serialized_msg, &cloud_msg); publish message pub.publish(cloud_msg); rclcpp::spin_some(node); rclcpp::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(100)); reader.close(); node.reset(); rclcpp::shutdown(); Code Publisher Explained break code piece piece. Header including [](https://github.com/ros-perception/point_cloud_transport): #include <point_cloud_transport/point_cloud_transport.hpp> Creates PointCloudTransport instance initializes Node shared pointer. Methods PointCloudTransport create point cloud publishers subscribers similar methods Node create generic publishers subscribers. point_cloud_transport::PointCloudTransport pct(node); PointCloudTransport method create publisher base topic “pct/point_cloud”. Depending plugins built, additional (per-plugin) topics derived base topic advertised. argument size publishing queue. point_cloud_transport::Publisher pub pct.advertise("pct/point_cloud", Publishes sensor_msgs::PointCloud2 message rosbag: sensor_msgs::msg::PointCloud2 cloud_msg; rosbag boiler plate populate cloud_msg publish message pub.publish(cloud_msg); spin node... rclcpp::spin_some(node); repeat... Running Publisher run my_publisher.cpp open terminal root workspace run source install/setup.bash ros2 run point_cloud_transport_tutorial publisher_test Writing Simple Subscriber section, create subscriber node, receives PointCloud2 messages prints number points Code Subscriber my_subscriber.cpp: #include <point_cloud_transport/point_cloud_transport.hpp> #include <rclcpp/rclcpp.hpp> #include <sensor_msgs/msg/point_cloud2.hpp> int main(int argc, char argv) rclcpp::init(argc, argv); auto node std::make_shared<rclcpp::Node>("point_cloud_subscriber"); point_cloud_transport::PointCloudTransport pct(node); point_cloud_transport::Subscriber pct_sub pct.subscribe( "pct/point_cloud", [node](const sensor_msgs::msg::PointCloud2::ConstSharedPtr msg) RCLCPP_INFO_STREAM( node->get_logger(), "Message received, number points msg->width msg->height); RCLCPP_INFO_STREAM(node->get_logger(), "Waiting point_cloud message..."); rclcpp::spin(node); rclcpp::shutdown(); return Code Subscriber Explained break code piece piece. Header including [](https://github.com/ros-perception/point_cloud_transport): #include <point_cloud_transport/point_cloud_transport.hpp> Initializes ROS node: rclcpp::init(argc, argv); auto node rclcpp::Node::make_shared("point_cloud_subscriber"); Creates PointCloudTransport instance initializes Node. Methods PointCloudTransport create point cloud publishers subscribers similar methods NodeHandle create generic publishers subscribers. point_cloud_transport::PointCloudTransport pct(node); PointCloudTransport method create subscriber base topic “pct/point_cloud”. argument size subscribing queue. argument tells subscriber execute lambda function message received. point_cloud_transport::Subscriber pct_sub pct.subscribe( "pct/point_cloud", [node](const sensor_msgs::msg::PointCloud2::ConstSharedPtr msg) RCLCPP_INFO_STREAM( node->get_logger(), "draco message received, number points msg->width msg->height); Select specific transport select specific transport TransportHint class. Creates TransportHint shared pointer. subscriber subscribe transport case “pct/point_cloud/draco”), raw “pct/point_cloud” topic. auto transport_hint std::make_shared<point_cloud_transport::TransportHints>("draco"); PointCloudTransport method create subscriber base topic “pct/point_cloud” add transport_hint variable argument. auto transport_hint std::make_shared<point_cloud_transport::TransportHints>("draco"); point_cloud_transport::Subscriber pct_sub pct.subscribe( "pct/point_cloud", [node](const sensor_msgs::msg::PointCloud2::ConstSharedPtr msg) RCLCPP_INFO_STREAM( node->get_logger(), "draco message received, number points msg->width msg->height); transport_hint.get()); Running Subscriber run my_subscriber.cpp, open terminal root workspace run source install/setup.bash ros2 run point_cloud_transport_tutorial subscriber_test --ros-args point_cloud_transport:=draco point_cloud_transport parameter read point_cloud_transport library. complexity parameters hidden library. Usage steps run reach study robot ROS1 infrastructure plugins. Create files describing robot system required REACH plugins URDF, SRDF, kinematics file, joint limits file, Create mesh model workpiece Note: origin model align kinematic base frame robot Create point cloud target points workpiece point cloud generated command line tool PCL pcl_mesh_sampling <workpiece_mesh>.ply <output_cloud>.pcd -n_samples <number samples> -leaf_size <leaf_size> -write_normals true Create configuration YAML file defining parameters reach study configuration interface plugins demo Run setup launch file ros2 launch reach_ros setup.launch robot_description_file:=<path_to_URDF> Run reach study analysis ros2 launch reach_ros start.launch robot_description_file:=<path_to_URDF> robot_description_semantic_file:=<path_to_SRDF> robot_description_kinematics_file:=<path_to_kinematics.yaml> robot_description_joint_limits_file:=<path_to_joint_limits.yaml> config_file:=<config_file.yaml> config_name:=<arbitrary_config> results_dir:=<arbitrary_results_directory> Evaluation Plugins Manipulability plugin MoveIt! calculate manipulability robot pose. Range Parameters: planning_group planning group evaluate manipulability robot pose jacobian_row_subset (optional) indices rows Jacobian evaluating manipulability. row indices correspond output space jacobian_row_subset: position manipulability Distance Penalty plugin MoveIt! collision environment calculate distance closest collision robot pose. distance score robot pose. Larger distance closest collision results higher pose score. Joint Penalty plugin MoveIt! robot model calculate robot pose score based pose deviates center joint range. Robot poses closer center joint range result higher pose scores. Solver plugin MoveIt! kinematics solvers collision checking produce collision aware solutions Parameters: planning_group planning group distance_threshold distance nearest collision invalidate solution. parameter set solutions distance nearest collision invalidated collision_mesh_filename (optional) file path collision mesh model workpiece, package:// ‘file://’ URI format collision_mesh_frame (optional) frame collision mesh attached left unspecified, collision mesh attached kinematic base frame planning_group touch_links links allowed contact collision mesh evaluation_plugin parameters) evaluation plugin score solution poses Discretized MoveIt! description: An interface library to connect ROS2 with the Soar cognitive architecture.
readme: soar_ros: ROS Interface Soar ROS2 package interface Soar cognitive architecture creating wrappers ROS2 messages handling Soar kernel continuos mode. Soar cognitive architecture developed University Michigan. field cognitive robotics projects, e.g. a drone robot. integration Soar ROS difficult complex projects, include multiple publishers, subscribers, services clients. main limitation orginates synchronous callback model Soar inspired creation wrapper. detailed explanation reason development package read software architecture. package relies forked version Soar. major include cmake-based build scons removal SWIG language interfaces. detailed comparison commit history fork. Features library developed targeting ROS Humble Ubuntu configurations tested. blocking Soar kernel Publisher Subscriber Service Client features supported, Action Server Client Multiple Soar agents Definition description public API API documentation generated rosdoc2, build documentation. Soar adds integers sends result ROS2 Service, based `soar_ros::Service` class. code [test/test\_soar\_ros.cpp](./test/test_soar_ros.cpp). {.sourceCode .cpp} class TestService public soar_ros::Service<example_interfaces::srv::AddTwoInts> public: TestService(sml::Agent agent, rclcpp::Node::SharedPtr node, const std::string topic) Service<example_interfaces::srv::AddTwoInts>(agent, node, topic) ~TestService() example_interfaces::srv::AddTwoInts::Response::SharedPtr parse(sml::Identifier override example_interfaces::srv::AddTwoInts::Response::SharedPtr response std::make_shared<example_interfaces::srv::AddTwoInts::Response>(); return response; void parse(example_interfaces::srv::AddTwoInts::Request::SharedPtr msg) override sml::Identifier getAgent()->GetInputLink(); sml::Identifier pId il->CreateIdWME("AddTwoInts"); pId->CreateIntWME("a", msg.get()->a); ROS package originally developed Andy Zelenak. Synapticon GmbH added examples, simulation, extended instructions easier installation, support containerization Docker. Table Contents Intention Overview Hardware Software Ubuntu ROS2 ROS2 Installation Synapticon Package Installation Demo Running Sudo (Optional) Isolated Environment (Docker) Docker Installation Synapticon Package Installation Demo Disclaimer Intention intention document provide instructions quickly Synapticon Devices ROS2 package Synapticon library. Additionally, order compatible Linux distributions, Docker images Overview subsections briefly demonstrate hardware software required Synapticon devices package. Hardware figure block diagram wiring setup Drives parameters configured OBLAC tools. Detailed instructions wiring diagrams devices Synapticon Web site documentation. package daisy chaining Synapticon drives order image Software demo, scenarios considered: Ubuntu installed system ROS2 (humble, rolling jazzy) Synapticon package installed system user run package isolated environment (Docker) Ubuntu ROS2 install ROS2 Ubuntu machine, follow steps ros.org install full version. minimal version installed; simulation desired, RViZ additionally installed. installation, configuration steps required. completeness demo, commands subsection copied website executed ROS2 installation. ROS2 Installation ensure locale supports UTF-8, run commands: sudo apt update sudo apt install locales sudo locale-gen en_US en_US.UTF-8 sudo update-locale LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 LANG=en_US.UTF-8 export LANG=en_US.UTF-8 Enable Ubuntu Universe repository: sudo apt install software-properties-common sudo add-apt-repository universe Add GPG key: sudo apt update sudo apt install curl sudo curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ros/rosdistro/master/ros.key /usr/share/keyrings/ros-archive-keyring.gpg Add repository sources: echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/ros-archive-keyring.gpg] http://packages.ros.org/ros2/ubuntu /etc/os-release echo $UBUNTU_CODENAME) main" sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ros2.list /dev/null Update apt cache: sudo apt update command updates packages system. Note commands work, skip step. sudo apt upgrade Finally, install ROS compilers (replace ROS_DISTRO desired ROS distribution humble, jazzy rolling): sudo apt install ros-ROS_DISTRO-desktop sudo apt install ros-dev-tools installation complete, add line /home/USER/.bashrc file (replace ROS_DISTRO desired ROS distribution humble, jazzy rolling): source /opt/ros/ROS_DISTRO/setup.bash order ROS2 interfere communication ports, set Domain (detailed information demo, ROS_DOMAIN_ID=1. add /home/$USER/.bashrc: export ROS_DOMAIN_ID=1 close reopen terminals. verify installation, open terminals run: ros2 run demo_nodes_cpp talker ros2 run demo_nodes_py listener nodes communicating, installation successful. Synapticon Package Installation OPTION Installing Source Create ROS2 workspace: mkdir ~/ros2_ws/src ~/ros2_ws/src Clone Synapticon package: git clone https://github.com/synapticon/synapticon_ros2_control Install build tools: sudo apt install python3-colcon-common-extensions Build package: ~/ros2_ws rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src colcon build Additionally, source workspace adding line /home/USER/.bashrc file line ROS installation sourced line: source /opt/ros/ROS_DISTRO/setup.bash: source /home/USER/ros2_ws/install/setup.bash OPTION Binary Installation required, add ROS repository occurs sudo apt install software-properties-common sudo add-apt-repository universe sudo apt update Install Synapticon package (replace ROS_DISTRO ROS distribution humble, jazzy rolling): sudo apt install ros-ROS_DISTRO-synapticon-ros2-control rosdep initialized updated: sudo rosdep init rosdep update Install dependencies: rosdep install synapticon_ros2_control package installed /opt/ros/ROS_DISTRO/share/synapticon_ros2_control/. VERIFICATION ethernet device drive connected, required. checked ifconfig command. Ethernet adapters start check master run slaves execute terminal (replace YOUR_ETHERNET_INTERFACE ifconfig). installed source: sudo Gazebo Harmonic heightmap spawner from ROS2 map
readme: heightmap_spawner ROS package converts occupancy grids nav2_map_server heightmap models Gazebo simulations. Features Occupancy Grid Conversion: Converts occupancy grid maps heightmaps simulation. Image Processing: Includes median filtering color inversion improve map accuracy. Configurable Thresholds: Adjustable parameters control image-to-heightmap transformation. Gazebo Integration: Automatically spawns processed heightmap Gazebo (gz-sim). Dependencies ROS Required running package ROS environment. Gazebo (gz-sim): simulation platform heightmap models spawned. Gazebo Transport Library (gz-transport): Facilitates communication Gazebo. OpenCV: image processing tasks filtering color inversion. nav2_map_server: occupancy grid maps converted. Installation Clone repository ROS workspace: git clone https://github.com/damanikjosh/heightmap_spawner.git Install required dependencies rosdep: rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src Build workspace colcon: colcon build Usage Launching Map launch file run map heightmap spawner action: ros2 launch heightmap_spawner example.launch.py Launching Spawner Node launch heightmap_spawner node spawner.launch.py file defining node launch files. Node Arguments Customize behavior heightmap spawner arguments: Argument Default Description save_path /tmp directory processed heightmap image saved. height vertical height spawned heightmap Gazebo. use_median_filtering true Enables median filtering reduce noise heightmap. use_color_inverse true Inverts colors image adjust height scaling. low_thresh threshold filtering occupancy grid image. high_thresh Upper threshold filtering occupancy grid image. License package licensed MIT License. LICENSE.txt file details.

readme: Network Bridge Network Bridge lightweight ROS2 node designed robust communication robotic systems arbitrary network protocols. Supporting UDP TCP protocols box, packages seamlessly bridges ROS2 topics networks, facilitating effective remote communications base station robotic systems, multiple robotic systems. Installation Simply clone repository ROS2 workspace build colcon build. Usage Demo TCP ros2 launch network_bridge tcp.launch.py ros2 topic pub /tcp1/MyDefaultTopic std_msgs/msg/String "data: 'Hello World'" ros2 topic echo /tcp2/MyDefaultTopic UDP ros2 launch network_bridge udp.launch.py ros2 topic pub /udp1/MyDefaultTopic std_msgs/msg/String "data: 'Hello World'" ros2 topic echo /udp2/MyDefaultTopic Configuration Simply setup network interface parameters list desired topics started. UDP cellular data, recommended setup VPN facilitate connection. note encryption occurs package. encryption, VPN. config/Udp1.yaml description parameters, TCP configuration files. Minimal configuration examples demonstrate robot sending message /gps/fix UDP basestation re-publish message. works seamlessly message types, long built sourced ends transmission. Robot /udp_sender: ros__parameters: UdpInterface: local_address: receive_port: remote_address: send_port: topics: "/gps/fix" Base Station /udp_receiver: ros__parameters: UdpInterface: local_address: receive_port: remote_address: send_port: Choice protocol UDP: UDP low-latency, high-throughput communications, occasional data loss tolerable. Ideal real-time telemetry data sensor streams. TCP: Opt TCP data integrity reliability critical. ensures control commands state transitions reliably delivered, potentially higher latency. Network protocols implemented pluginlib plugins, allowing creation arbitrary interfaces abstract class include/network_interfaces/network_interface_base.hpp. interface send receive bytes theoretically implemented, including protocols point-to-point communication, ZMQ. opening pull request implement network interface. Tuning node launched logger level DEBUG, information tuning compression, rate stale message parameters. message receiving side output number bytes received, decompressed size bytes transmission delay. Acknowledgements package developed Indy Autonomous Challenge Purdue Racing team. Inspiration mqtt_client (https://github.com/ika-rwth-aachen/mqtt_client/).

description: A ROS2 package for transmitting NumPy ndarrays between ROS2 nodes.
readme: ROS2 NDArray Message ROS2 package transmitting NumPy ndarrays ROS2 nodes. Features Custom ROS2 message type numpy.ndarray Bi-directional conversion NumPy arrays ROS2 messages Multi-dimensional array support data types Type-safe Python utilities full type hints Requirements ROS2 Humble higher Python Installation ~/ros2_ws/src git clone https://github.com/Geson-anko/numpy_ndarray_msgs.git colcon build --packages-select numpy_ndarray_msgs source install/setup.sh Install Python Utility Package pip install ros2-ndarray-msg-utils Usage Python Utility Package import numpy ndarray_msg_utils import to_ros_msg, from_ros_msg, NDArray rclpy.clock import ROSClock Convert NumPy array ROS2 message array np.array([[1, dtype=np.float32) Type Hint msg: NDArray msg to_ros_msg(array) Header msg to_ros_msg(array, timestamp=ROSClock().now(), frame_id="array_frame") Convert NumPy array restored from_ros_msg(msg) PyTorch Support pip install "ros2-ndarray-msg-utils[torch]" import torch ndarray_msg_utils.torch import to_ros_msg, from_ros_msg Convert PyTorch tensor ROS2 message tensor torch.tensor([[1, dtype=torch.float32) msg to_ros_msg(tensor) Convert PyTorch tensor restored from_ros_msg(msg) License MIT License

description: A Python package which creates simple interactive menus on the command line.
readme:
Simple Terminal Menu Overview simple-term-menu creates simple menus interactive command line programs. offer choice options user. Menu entries selected arrow, emacs (C-n/C-p) keys. module terminfo database detect terminal features automatically disables styles Linux macOS supported. Breaking version update version breaking TerminalMenu constructor takes keyword-only arguments parameter menu entries). easier add parameters future releases well-arranged parameter list. command line interface revised. separate words consistently rearranges short options. important short options save free letters future releases. version multi_select_key parameter named multi_select_keys takes iterable keys default space andtab multi-select keys. toggle selected items search mode. shortcut_parentheses_highlight_style parameter renamed shortcut_brackets_highlight_style consistent multi_select_cursor_brackets_style parameter. Installation simple-term-menu PyPI Python installed pip: python3 pip install simple-term-menu Arch Linux derivatives, install simple-term-menu AUR: yay python-simple-term-menu find self-contained executables 64-bit Linux distributions macOS High Sierra newer releases page. created PyInstaller require glibc Linux fine Linux system). Usage Create menu default style Create instance class TerminalMenu pass menu entries list strings constructor. Call show method output menu wait keyboard input: #!/usr/bin/env python3 simple_term_menu import TerminalMenu def main(): options ["entry "entry "entry terminal_menu TerminalMenu(options) menu_entry_index terminal_menu.show() print(f"You selected {options[menu_entry_index]}!") "__main__": main() output select menu entry arrow keys (vim motions) accept choice hitting enter cancel menu escape, <Ctrl>-C. show returns selected menu entry index menu canceled. pass optional title TerminalMenu constructor menu. title simple string, multiline string newlines) list strings. applies status_bar parameter, places status bar menu. callable status_bar parameter takes selected entry returns status bar string. Styling pass styling arguments TerminalMenu constructor. style tuple keyword strings. keywords accepted: bg_black bg_blue bg_cyan bg_gray bg_green bg_purple bg_red bg_yellow fg_black fg_blue fg_cyan fg_gray fg_green fg_purple fg_red fg_yellow bold italics standout underline alter styles: menu_cursor_style: style shown cursor. default style ("fg_red", "bold"). menu_highlight_style: style selected menu entry. default style ("standout",). search_highlight_style: style matched search strings. default style ("fg_black", "bg_yellow", "bold"). shortcut_key_highlight_style: style shortcut keys. default style ("fg_blue",). shortcut_brackets_highlight_style: style brackets enclosing shortcut keys. default style ("fg_gray",). status_bar_style: style status bar menu. default style ("fg_yellow", "bg_black"). multi_select_cursor_style: style cursor pins selected entry multi-selection. default style ("fg_yellow", "bold"). style excludes brackets multi_select_cursor_brackets_style: style brackets multi_select_cursor default style ("fg_gray",). setting menu_cursor define cursor disable default cursor parameter multi_select_cursor customizes multi-select cursor default Searching simple_term_menu built-in search feature filter shown menu entries. default key activate search mode Vim, terminal programs). prefer search key, pass search_key parameter TerminalMenu constructor. passed activate search letter key. cursor motions mode. <Ctrl-j> <Ctrl-k> search mode supports Python regex syntax. Visit Python documentation details. String parts menu entries match search pattern highlighted. search_highlight_style parameter adjust highlight style liking. default, search case insensitive. Set search_case_sensitive True prefer case sensitive search line. Pass show_search_hint=True TerminalMenu constructor activate search hint search line (Press search)). Shortcuts define shortcuts selected menu entries prepending single character enclosed square brackets Pass shortcut_key_highlight_style and/or shortcut_brackets_highlight_style TerminalMenu constructor change default highlight style shortcuts. default, show method returns shortcut key pressed. selection jump shortcut target, pass exit_on_shortcut=False TerminalMenu constructor. configured search activated letter key, shortcut feature disabled. Pass show_shortcut_hints=True TerminalMenu constructor display shortcut hints status bar long menus scrolling). Additionally pass show_shortcut_hints_in_status_bar=False prefer shortcut hints menu title. Shortcuts Create menu fruits letter shortcut key: #!/usr/bin/env python3 import simple_term_menu import TerminalMenu def main(): fruits apple", banana", orange"] terminal_menu TerminalMenu(fruits, title="Fruits") menu_entry_index terminal_menu.show() "__main__": main() Custom accept keys default key accept menu item enter. Pass accept_keys parameter tuple keys strings) TerminalMenu constructor set accept keys. Custom accept keys plain ascii letters ascii letters modifier key (prepend ctrl- alt- ascii letter). chosen_accept_key property TerminalMenu instance query accept key pressed user. aware combinations modifier ascii keys work depending terminal emulator graphical user interface. addition, combinations generate existing keys ctrl-m enter carriage return). Custom accept keys #!/usr/bin/env python3 simple_term_menu import TerminalMenu def main(): terminal_menu TerminalMenu(["entry "entry "entry accept_keys=("enter", "alt-d", "ctrl-i")) menu_entry_index terminal_menu.show() print(terminal_menu.chosen_accept_key) "__main__": main() Multi-select Pass multi_select=True TerminalMenu constructor enable multi-select mode. Press space tab arbitrary menu item add selection. Press enter configured accept_key) add selected entry item selection return show method usual. multi-select mode, show method returns sorted tuple selected menu indices single int. chosen_menu_entries property tuple menu entry strings setting multi_select_keys define set keys toggle selected item. passing show_multi_select_hint=True multi-select mode hint shown status bar. don’t accept_key select highlighted item pass multi_select_select_on_accept=False. menu item explicitly selected, highlighted menu item added selection pass multi_select_empty_ok=True. optional list iterable object) preselected_entries passed items selected menu displayed. list composed integers representing indexes menu_entries list, strings matching elements menu_entries. Integers strings mixed. Multi-select #!/usr/bin/env python3 simple_term_menu import TerminalMenu def main(): terminal_menu TerminalMenu( ["dog", "cat", "mouse", "squirrel"], multi_select=True, show_multi_select_hint=True, menu_entry_indices terminal_menu.show() print(menu_entry_indices) print(terminal_menu.chosen_menu_entries) "__main__": main() Preview window simple-term-menu show preview menu entry. Pass preview_command TerminalMenu constructor activate optional feature. preview_command takes command string executed subprocess Python callable converts menu entry string preview output. command string pattern replaced current menu entry string. menu entry additional data component (separated passed preview command. literal simply append data component), preview window disabled entry. additional keyword argument preview_size control height preview window. fraction complete terminal height (default: width set, complete width terminal window. Pass preview_title string choice customize preview window title (default: "preview") preview_border=False deactivate border preview window deactivates title string). Preview commands allowed generate ANSI escape color codes. Preview examples Create menu files current directory preview contents bat command: #!/usr/bin/env python3 import simple_term_menu import TerminalMenu def list_files(directory="."): return (file file os.listdir(directory) os.path.isfile(os.path.join(directory, file))) def main(): terminal_menu TerminalMenu(list_files(), preview_command="bat --color=always preview_size=0.75) menu_entry_index terminal_menu.show() "__main__": main() file preview Pygments api: #!/usr/bin/env python3 import pygments import formatters, highlight, lexers pygments.util import ClassNotFound simple_term_menu import TerminalMenu def highlight_file(filepath): open(filepath, file_content f.read() lexer lexers.get_lexer_for_filename(filepath, stripnl=False, stripall=False) ClassNotFound: lexer lexers.get_lexer_by_name("text", stripnl=False, stripall=False) formatter formatters.TerminalFormatter(bg="dark") dark light highlighted_file_content highlight(file_content, lexer, formatter) return highlighted_file_content def list_files(directory="."): return (file file os.listdir(directory) os.path.isfile(os.path.join(directory, file))) def main(): terminal_menu TerminalMenu(list_files(), preview_command=highlight_file, preview_size=0.75) menu_entry_index terminal_menu.show() "__main__": main() Preview active pane running tmux session session ids appended menu entries separator): #!/usr/bin/env python3 import subprocess simple_term_menu import TerminalMenu def list_tmux_sessions(): tmux_command_output subprocess.check_output( ["tmux", "list-sessions", "-F#{session_id}:#{session_name}"], universal_newlines=True tmux_sessions line tmux_command_output.split("
"): line line.strip() line: continue session_id, session_name tuple(line.split(":")) tmux_sessions.append((session_name, session_id)) return tmux_sessions def main(): terminal_menu TerminalMenu( ("|".join(session) session list_tmux_sessions()), preview_command="tmux capture-pane preview_size=0.75, menu_entry_index terminal_menu.show() "__main__": main() Skipping empty entries constructor parameter skip_empty_entries flag --skip-empty-entries interpret empty string menu entries empty menu entry skipped iterating entries). considered empty menu entry independently skip_empty_entries parameter. simple_term_menu import TerminalMenu def main(): options ["entry "entry "add", "edit"] ["entry "entry "add", "edit"] terminal_menu TerminalMenu(options, skip_empty_entries=True) TerminalMenu(options) menu_entry_index terminal_menu.show() print(f"You selected {options[menu_entry_index]}!") "__main__": main() Localization Text modification constructor parameters show_search_hint_text show_multi_select_hint_text modify texts. placeholder {key} search key show_search_hint_text {accept_keys} {multi_select_keys} show_multi_select_hint_text appropriately. Additional settings TerminalMenu constructor takes additional parameters change menu behavior: clear_menu_on_exit: bool menu cleared show method. Defaults True. clear_screen: bool screen cleared menu shown. Defaults False. cursor_index: initially selected item index. cycle_cursor: bool menu cursor cycles menu reached. Defaults True. quit_keys: iterable keys quit terminal menu. Defaults ("escape", raise_error_on_interrupt: Set True reraise Keyboard interrupts pressing <Ctrl-c>). Defaults False. status_bar_below_preview: Position status bar preview window (default positioning Command line program simple-term-menu terminal program shell scripts. exit code script 1-based index description: Turtlebot4 setup scripts
readme: Turtlebot4 Setup Setup scripts tools TurtleBot Raspberry Visit TurtleBot User Manual details. Create® updated firmware; older versions firmware compatible ROS Jazzy. Create image manually Follow instructions create Turtlebot4 image manually. Create Ubuntu Image install Raspberry Imager. Insert card run Raspberry Imager. Follow instructions install Ubuntu Server (64-bit) card. Ensure Raspberry powered inserting flashed card. set Raspberry connecting network Ethernet keyboard HDMI monitor micro HDMI cable. Ethernet Setup Connect Raspberry Network Ethernet cable. Boot Raspberry Find Raspberry Pi’s router’s portal. SSH Raspberry address. ssh ubuntu@xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx default login ubuntu password ubuntu. prompted change password. HDMI Setup Connect keyboard Raspberry USB. Connect monitor Raspberry HDMI0 port. Boot Raspberry default login ubuntu password ubuntu. prompted change password. Manually configure Wi-Fi logged Raspberry configure Wi-Fi: sudo nano /etc/netplan/50-cloud-init.yaml Add lines: wifis: wlan0: optional: true access-points: "YOUR_WIFI_SSID": password: "YOUR_WIFI_PASSWORD" dhcp4: true Note: Ensure wifis: aligned existing ethernets: line. indentations spaces. tabs. Reboot Raspberry connected Wi-Fi. Find Raspberry Pi’s router’s portal. SSH Raspberry address. ssh ubuntu@xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx Download run setup script wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/turtlebot/turtlebot4_setup/jazzy/scripts/turtlebot4_setup.sh bash script automatically install ROS Jazzy, TurtleBot packages, important apt packages. configure RPi4 work TurtleBot complete, RPi4 rebooted sudo reboot. run turtlebot4-setup configure robot setup tool.

This package provides a execution tracing library.
readme: YAETS: Execution Tracing System YAETS library designed trace function execution asynchronously, combined Python tools analyze results Gantt charts histograms. Features Function tracing TraceSession, TraceGuard, NamedSharedTrace, TraceRegistry classes. Asynchronous logging trace events prevent performance overhead. Python scripts visualize traces Gantt charts analyze timing gaps traces histograms. Table Contents YAETS: Execution Tracing System Features Table Contents Installation Requirements Building Library ROS Building Library ROS Usage Tracing Library Advanced Tracing NamedSharedTrace TraceRegistry NamedSharedTrace TraceRegistry Manage Shared Traces Macros NamedSharedTrace Works Key Methods Python Gantt Chart Script Usage Options Python Histogram Script ellapsed time Usage Options Python Histogram Script execution time Usage Options Building Running Tests Tracing Session Code trace: Running graphs License Installation Requirements CMake building project Python running scripts Matplotlib visualizing data Python Building Library ROS Clone repository navigate project directory: git clone https://github.com/fmrico/yaets.git yaets Build library CMake: mkdir build build cmake Install library: sudo install Building Library ROS Clone repository navigate project directory: my_ros_ws/src git clone https://github.com/fmrico/yaets.git Build library Colcon: colcon build Usage Tracing Library YAETS simple library tracing function execution times. key classes TraceSession TraceGuard. #include <yaets/tracing.hpp> void example_function(yaets::TraceSession& session) TRACE_EVENT(session); Automatically traces function entry exit function logic int main() yaets::TraceSession session("trace_output.log"); example_function(session); session.stop(); Stop session flush trace events return Advanced Tracing NamedSharedTrace TraceRegistry scenarios trace shared events multiple parts application, NamedSharedTrace TraceRegistry. NamedSharedTrace NamedSharedTrace class start stop traces parts code, shared trace #include <yaets/tracing.hpp> int main() yaets::TraceSession session("shared_trace_output.log"); yaets::NamedSharedTrace trace1(session, "shared_event"); trace1.start(); Perform operations trace1.end(); session.stop(); return TraceRegistry Manage Shared Traces TraceRegistry, register manage multiple NamedSharedTrace instances allowing start stop traces application referencing NamedSharedTrace objects. Initialize TraceRegistry: Register traces IDs centralized management. Start Stop Traces macros simplify starting stopping traces. #include <yaets/tracing.hpp> int main() yaets::TraceSession session("registry_trace_output.log"); Register traces unique IDs yaets::TraceRegistry::getInstance().registerTrace("trace1", session); yaets::TraceRegistry::getInstance().registerTrace("trace2", session); Start traces yaets::TraceRegistry::getInstance().startTrace("trace1"); Operations trace1 yaets::TraceRegistry::getInstance().endTrace("trace1"); yaets::TraceRegistry::getInstance().startTrace("trace2"); Operations trace2 yaets::TraceRegistry::getInstance().endTrace("trace2"); session.stop(); return Macros NamedSharedTrace streamline usage NamedSharedTrace TraceRegistry, YAETS macros initializing, starting, stopping traces simplifies code readability reduces call methods TraceRegistry. include <yaets/tracing.hpp> int main() yaets::TraceSession session("macro_trace_output.log"); Initialize traces SHARED_TRACE_INIT(session, "macro_trace1"); SHARED_TRACE_INIT(session, "macro_trace2"); Start stop traces SHARED_TRACE_START("macro_trace1"); Operations macro_trace1 SHARED_TRACE_END("macro_trace1"); SHARED_TRACE_START("macro_trace2"); Operations macro_trace2 SHARED_TRACE_END("macro_trace2"); session.stop(); return class SensorDriverNode public rclcpp::Node void produce_data() SHARED_TRACE_START("brake_process"); waste_time(shared_from_this(), sensor_msgs::msg::Image image_msg; pub_->publish(image_msg); class BrakeActuatorNode public rclcpp::Node void react_obstacle(vision_msgs::msg::Detection3D::SharedPtr msg) waste_time(shared_from_this(), SHARED_TRACE_END("brake_process"); int main(int argc, char argv[]) SHARED_TRACE_INIT(session, "brake_process"); Works TraceSession: Manages tracing session writes events asynchronously log file. TraceGuard: Automatically traces start function. created start function destroyed function exits, capturing execution time. NamedSharedTrace: create named traces started stopped independently parts code. TraceRegistry: centralized management NamedSharedTrace instances, allowing register, start, stop traces Key Methods TraceSession::TraceSession(const std::string filename): Initializes session specifies output file trace events. TraceSession::stop(): Stops session writes remaining events file. TraceGuard::TraceGuard(TraceSession session, const std::string function_name): Captures function start time. TraceGuard::~TraceGuard(): Logs time registers trace event. NamedSharedTrace::start(): Begins trace event. NamedSharedTrace::end(): Ends trace event logs TraceSession. TraceRegistry::registerTrace(const std::string& TraceSession& session): Registers trace unique TraceRegistry::startTrace(const std::string& Starts trace TraceRegistry::endTrace(const std::string& Ends trace Python Gantt Chart Script Python script gantt.py visualize traced functions Gantt chart. Usage Ensure trace log file generated program trace_output.log). Run gantt.py script: python3 scripts/gantt.py trace_output.log --max_traces altenativelly ros2 run yaest gantt.py trace_output.log --max_traces Options --max_traces: maximum number trace events display Gantt chart. resulting Gantt chart shows function’s execution times, allowing visualize sequence duration function calls. Python Histogram Script ellapsed time Python script elaspsed_histogram.py analyzes intervals function executions visualizes histogram. Usage Run elaspsed_histogram.py script, function analyze: python3 scripts/elaspsed_histogram.py trace_output.log --function example_function --bins altenativelly ros2 run yaest elaspsed_histogram.py trace_output.log --function example_function --bins Options --function: function execution intervals analyze. --bins: number bins histogram control resolution). tool helps understand frequently functions called patterns execution intervals. Python Histogram Script execution time Python script execution_histogram.py analyzes execution time function visualizes histogram. Usage Run execution_histogram.py script, function analyze: python3 scripts/execution_histogram.py trace_output.log --function example_function --bins altenativelly ros2 run yaest execution_histogram.py trace_output.log --function example_function --bins Options --function: function execution execution time analyze. --bins: number bins histogram control resolution). Building Running Tests YAETS includes unit tests verify functionality tracing library. build run tests: build directory, run commands: tests cmake ./yaets_test colcon test run tests tests/yaets_test.cpp verify correctness tracing system. Tracing Session graphs initial document instructions: Code trace: #include <fstream> #include "yaets/tracing.hpp" #include "rclcpp/rclcpp.hpp" #include "std_msgs/msg/int32.hpp" namespace std::chrono_literals; std::placeholders::_1; yaets::TraceSession session("session1.log"); class ProducerNode public rclcpp::Node public: ProducerNode() Node("producer_node") pub_1_ create_publisher<std_msgs::msg::Int32>("topic_1", pub_2_ create_publisher<std_msgs::msg::Int32>("topic_2", timer_ create_wall_timer(1ms, std::bind(&ProducerNode::timer_callback, void timer_callback() TRACE_EVENT(session); message_.data pub_1_->publish(message_); message_.data pub_2_->publish(message_); private: rclcpp::Publisher<std_msgs::msg::Int32>::SharedPtr pub_1_, pub_2_; rclcpp::TimerBase::SharedPtr timer_; std_msgs::msg::Int32 message_; class ConsumerNode public rclcpp::Node public: ConsumerNode() Node("consumer_node") create_subscription<std_msgs::msg::Int32>( "topic_2", std::bind(&ConsumerNode::cb_2, create_subscription<std_msgs::msg::Int32>( "topic_1", std::bind(&ConsumerNode::cb_1, timer_ create_wall_timer(10ms, std::bind(&ConsumerNode::timer_callback, void cb_1(const std_msgs::msg::Int32::SharedPtr msg) TRACE_EVENT(session); waste_time(200us); void cb_2(const std_msgs::msg::Int32::SharedPtr msg) TRACE_EVENT(session); waste_time(200us); void timer_callback() TRACE_EVENT(session); waste_time(3ms); void waste_time(const rclcpp::Duration duration) auto start start duration); private: rclcpp::Subscription<std_msgs::msg::Int32>::SharedPtr rclcpp::Subscription<std_msgs::msg::Int32>::SharedPtr rclcpp::TimerBase::SharedPtr timer_; int main(int argc, char argv[]) rclcpp::init(argc, argv); auto node_pub std::make_shared<ProducerNode>(); auto node_sub std::make_shared<ConsumerNode>(); rclcpp::executors::MultiThreadedExecutor executor; executor.add_node(node_pub); executor.add_node(node_sub); executor.spin(); rclcpp::shutdown(); return Running graphs Stress system significant results: stress-ng $(nproc) terminal ros2 run yaets executors terminal Stop processes ros2 run yaest gantt.py ./session1.log --max_traces ros2 run yaest elaspsed_histogram.py ../session1.log --function ConsumerNode::timer_callback --bins graphs: License YAETS licensed Apache License LICENSE file details.
